blocks.content: Across town, Daisy sat cross-legged on her bed with the dress back on its hanger and the house quiet.
He leaned in. The song ended. They smiled at each other like idiots in the middle of the gym.
She turned it over. All of it.
She looked at it. Didn't answer.
i saw you two. just checking in
She put the laptop on the bed beside her and stared at the ceiling. She knew what Savannah would say. She needed a minute without someone else's clarity applied to this one.
He'd leaned in. She'd leaned in. The song ended at the exact worst second. And then they'd smiled — that was the part she couldn't stop thinking about. Not the almost. The smile after.
The AIM chimed again. She reached for the laptop. Not Savannah.
